mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate elevated above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations and raises corporate borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 50k persons in single month
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of months following PAYEMS declines > 50k, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 1200k units signals demand destruction in residential construction and related consumer durables, reducing GDP growth expectations and equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1331k units as of August 2007
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of periods when HOUST < 1200k, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index decline combined with elevated unemployment signals supply-side weakness and demand insufficiency, producing negative equity returns as profit margins compress and growth expectations fall over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.5% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 114.43 and UNRATE at 4.7% as of August 2007
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when both INDPRO and UNRATE conditions are met simultaneously, or joint interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth deceleration below 2% year-over-year combined with rising unemployment signals consumer demand weakness that reduces corporate revenue growth and equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 380230M and UNRATE at 4.7% as of September 2007
regime_hint: consumer demand recession with labor market stress
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in > 60% of instances when both RSAFS and UNRATE conditions are jointly met, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI index rising above 2.5% year-over-year forces Federal Reserve to maintain or raise rates, compressing equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced real earnings growth over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 207.738 and FEDFUNDS at 4.94% as of August-September 2007
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when both inflation and rate conditions are met, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth trajectory measured quarterly signals underlying economic momentum; sustained growth above 2% year-over-year supports equity valuations and positive forward returns, while growth below 1.5% signals recession risk and negative returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 growth rate and 60-day SPX returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation reverses sign in post-2008 period.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.8% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and spending, triggering negative equity returns as earnings expectations decline over the next 5 trading days through immediate sentiment shock.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive in > 65% of instances following UNRATE increases > 0.3pp, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
